Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Embracing Bayesian methods in clinical trials 46 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Can It Resolve DOOM? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Explore the Hidden World of Sand 134 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Meow.camera 112 upvotes, 26 comments
  5. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 39 upvotes, 6 comments
  6. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate on a Brother printer with Certbot 160 upvotes, 44 comments
  7. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 55 upvotes, 12 comments
  8. Browser-based SFX synthesizer using WASM/Zig 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 270 upvotes, 108 comments
  10. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 90 upvotes, 34 comments
  11. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 294 upvotes, 149 comments
  12. A Faster Alternative to Jq 348 upvotes, 218 comments
  13. ISBN Visualization – Annas Archive 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Capability-Based Security for Redox: Namespace and CWD as Capabilities 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Make macOS consistently bad (unironically) 130 upvotes, 81 comments
  16. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 151 upvotes, 109 comments
  17. Hold on to Your Hardware 515 upvotes, 428 comments
  18. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 482 upvotes, 399 comments
  19. AI got the blame for the Iran school bombing. The truth is more worrying 249 upvotes, 196 comments
  20. Ask HN: Founders of estonian e-businesses – is it worth it? 48 upvotes, 29 comments
  21. Schedule tasks on the web 272 upvotes, 220 comments
  22. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 50 upvotes, 31 comments
  23. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 377 upvotes, 323 comments
  24. Some uncomfortable truths about AI coding agents 48 upvotes, 36 comments
  25. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 145 upvotes, 138 comments
  26. Solving Semantle with the Wrong Embeddings 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  27. Show HN: Open-Source Animal Crossing–Style UI for Claude Code Agents 23 upvotes, 16 comments
  28. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 51 upvotes, 75 comments
  29. Should QA exist? 67 upvotes, 107 comments
  30. Telnyx package compromised on PyPI 37 upvotes, 58 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 28 March 2026. Source here.
